feedback.text: Buying a video game and removing access after an undefined period of time at the time of purchase is completely immoral. Today’s publishers are doing as if the problem was impossible to solve and they had no choice, whereas the problem has already been solved since the 90s with the arrival of certain multi-player games such as Doom, and Quake, which give players the opportunity to host their own private servers. This is no longer a problem, just the infinite advices of publishers who want to be able to deny access to a game that is too old, to be able to sell the next opus at full price to the same players. Immoral, and almost illegal.
